rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAGILAVY FAMILY TRUST ESTABLISHED JUNE 5, 1989 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: David Magilavys Demurrer To Beryl Magilavys Petition
rulings.Rulings: The demurrer of Respondent David Magilavy is sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth and fifth causes of action, which assert no cognizable claim and seek no available remedy, but only seek to prevent Respondent from even asserting certain affirmative defenses, the merits of which the Court cannot adjudicate before they are asserted. The demurrer is overruled as to the remaining causes of action on the basis of Petitioner's representation that she is making claims against Respondent exclusively in his capacity as trustee (which is consistent with the language of the petition) and is making no claims against him in any other capacity. The Court construes the petition as being so limited, and will hold Petitioner to her representation in this respect. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, April 12, 2017.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAGILAVY FAMILY TRUST ESTABLISHED JUNE 5, 1989 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike David Magilavy'S Demurrer To Beryl Magilavy'S Petition
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner's motion to strike Respondent David Magilary's Demurrer is denied. The demurrer was not improper. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise otehr parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wedndesday, April 12, 2017.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAGILAVY FAMILY TRUST ESTABLISHED JUNE 5, 1989 TRUST
rulings.Rulings: The demurrer of Respondent Maureen Magilavy is sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth and fifth causes of action, which assert no cognizable claim and seek no available remedy, but only seek to prevent Respondent from even asserting certain affirmative defenses, the merits of which the Court cannot adjudicate before they are asserted. The demurrer to the fifteenth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend because only the trustee can be compelled to address the internal affairs of the trust. The demurrer to the sixteenth cause of action is overruled because Respondent is arguably a necessary or appropriate party to some of the requested relief by virtue of her position in the chain of title as to some of the real property as to which injunctive relief is sought. In all other respects, the demurrer is sustained with 15 days leave to amend to allege facts that would support each element of aider and abettor liability as to each of the remaining claims. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, April 12, 2017.
